_readme: EXAMPLE selector map for fidelity.mjs. Copy the shape into <repo>/scripts/fidelity.map.json (project config — versioned with the code, NOT with the skill). One key per surface. The engine AUTO-WALKS the whole subtree from each root, so you only declare the ROOT selector + setup per side; `nodes[]` is optional and only pins tricky pairings. Reveal/verify selectors with `--probe`. Prefer a stable attribute over structural nth-child: a prototype runtime's data-* (e.g. DataChisel stamps data-dc-tpl on every templated node) on the design side, a data-testid you add to the component on the built side.

prototype.setup: [ {click|hover|waitFor|fill|evaluate} ] — steps to reach the POPULATED state

built.setup: [ steps ] to reach + seed the surface

<key>.figmaNote: when source=figma there is no prototype block; extract the reference tree via MCP into a JSON (references/figma-extract.md) and pass --reference. `built` is still measured live.
<key>.nodes: OPTIONAL overrides — [{ name, refSel, builtSel, self?, states? }]. Plain override re-anchors a SUBTREE (paths relative to the anchor) — good for a single wrapper. `self: true` measures ONLY the two matched elements (no child walk), depth-independent — use when the built markup nests the node many wrappers deeper than the design (the engine prints a ⚠ 'shapes diverge' warning telling you to switch to self pairs; have the LLM map the nodes, then pin them here). `states`: ["hover","focus"] re-measures with the state triggered on the anchor. Overrides/states need the rendered design (prototype path).

datasets/card._verified: Design values measured live from OkLabel.dc.html (chromium headless): card r=12px pad=16px · tile 34px r=9px bg rgba(24,188,242,.13) icon rgb(24,188,242) · name 13.5px/600 · subtitle 11px muted capitalize · stat mono 15px/600 · stat-label 10.5px · caps-label 10.5px/600 ls .04em · chip 11px r=20px bg-muted · footer-meta 11px · attach-link 12px/600 rgb(37,99,235). The prototype tile radius (9px) contradicts the adopted rounded-tile token (11px) — a Phase 9 baseline decision, not an arbitrary.
datasets/card._nodesNote: OPTIONAL. Omit for the normal case (engine auto-walks from the root). Add overrides ONLY when the built markup wraps differently from the design (e.g. shadcn inserts a <div>/Slot), which shifts structural paths and floods missing+extra. Each override re-anchors a subtree by selector on each side, paired by paths RELATIVE to that anchor — jumping over the wrapper. Requires the rendered design (prototype path), not --reference/--cache.
